LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF ALBERT LUJAN'S MOTION FOR LEAVE TO FILE SECOND AMENDED AND SUPPLEMENTAL COMPLAINT NO APPEARANCES. THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: GRANTED. CALIFORNIA COURTS LIBERALLY ALLOW AMENDMENTS TO PLEADINGS ABSENT A SHOWING OF PREJUDICE. (DOUGLAS V. SUP. CT. (1989) 215 CAL.APP.3D 155, 158; CCP 576.) IF THE AMENDED COMPLAINT IS BELIEVED DEFECTIVE, CCP 430.41 IS TO BE FOLLOWED. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: RICHARD B. ULMER, CLERK: E. LARNAUTI, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600 (EMAIL: MARIA.TORREANO@GMAIL.COM) (302/RBU)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Dec-05-2019 Continued To Jan-29-20 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Nov-25-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
, Esq. of T: for and moving party;
, Esq. of T: for and opposing party.

RULING: The tentative ruling was contested and argued. At the conclusion of arguments, the Court adopted the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT EDUARDO RALLO's DEMURRER TO THE 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT (TAC) is sustained without leave to amend on statute of limitations grounds.

Kimomex Corporation operated a Mexican grocery from 2007 to 2010. Plaintiff Albert Lujan was Kimomex's CEO, CFO and secretary. (TAC 7:11-15.) Lujan was also Kimomex's responsible managing employee (RME), making him personally liable for its unpaid sales, employment and income taxes. (Id.) Lujan pleads that "commencing in 2014, taxing authorities have seized on" him "to satisfy these liabilities based on his status as RME." (Id. at 9:12-15.)
On August 23, 2019, Lujan filed his TAC; it pleads a single cause of action, for "indemnity and contribution." It is undisputed that the statute of limitations for these claims is two years. (Am. States Ins. Co. v. National Fire Ins. Co. of Hartford (2011) 202 Cal.App.4th 692, 698-99.) Lujan argues that "continuing injury" extends the statutory period, because he is paying down Kimomex's past taxes over time. However, the injury was sustained when the tax obligations accrued and Lujan became liable for them, not as Lujan pays them off years later. The statute of limitations has long since run.

The cases Lujan relies on are not to the contrary. In Howard Jarvis Taxpayers Assn. v. City of La Habra (1001) 25 Cal.4th 809, 819, a tax was imposed into the future, not just assessed in the past. In Aryeh v. Canon Business Solutions, Inc. (2013) 55 Cal.4th 1185, defendant continued to make excess charges during a limitations period.

Order signed in open court.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Jr.; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Maria A. Torreano CSR#8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File Second Amended And Supplemental Comlaint
Off Calendar May-21-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF ALBERT LUJAN'S MOTION FOR LEAVE TO FILE SECOND AMENDED AND SUPPLEMENTAL COMPLAINT

No appearances. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: GRANTED. California courts liberally allow amendments to pleadings absent a showing of prejudice. (Douglas v. Sup. Ct. (1989) 215 Cal.App.3d 155, 158; CCP 576.) If the amended complaint is believed defective, CCP 430.41 is to be followed. Order signed. Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR 8600 (Email: maria.torreano@gmail.com) (302/RBU)
